resposta:
solution: 
```
class Solution:
    def resultingString(self, s: str) -> str:
        while len(s) > 1 and s[0] + 1 == s[1]:
            s = s[1:]
        return s
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses a while loop to repeatedly remove the leftmost pair of adjacent characters in the string that are consecutive in the alphabet. The loop continues until the string has less than two characters or no more consecutive characters can be removed. The solution has a high efficiency due to its simplicity and the fact that it only iterates over the string once.
